---
title: "STA313 A2"
author: "Tala, Jinny"
date: "2026-03-06"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r, load data sets. echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
jobs <- read.csv('Jobs.csv')
participants <- read.csv('Participants.csv')
pubs <- read.csv ('Pubs.csv')
rest <- read.csv('Restaurants.csv')
finjour <- read.csv('Data/FinancialJournal.csv')
apartments <- read.csv('Apartments.csv')
logs <- read.csv("Data/ParticipantStatusLogs1.csv")
travel_data <- read.csv("Data/TravelJournal.csv")
```

```{r, load libraries, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(data.table)
```

```{r -  EDA #1 }
# Exploring how often social spaces were used across the observation period: 
social_logs <- logs %>%
  filter(currentMode %in% c("AtRestaurant", "AtRecreation"))

# Count total visits per venue type
visits_summary <- social_logs %>%
  mutate(venueType = ifelse(str_detect(currentMode, "Restaurant"), "Restaurant", "Pub")) %>%
  group_by(venueType) %>%
  summarise(total_visitors = n_distinct(participantId), .groups = "drop")

# Bar chart
ggplot(visits_summary, aes(x = venueType, y = total_visitors, fill = venueType)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Visits to Social Venues",
    x = "Venue Type",
    y = "Number of Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pub" = "darkred", "Restaurant" = "orange")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# kinda useless plot, results ignore temporal patterns, repeat visits, length of time spent at venue
```

```{r - EDA #2}
# Where do people spend most of their time?
mode_counts <- logs %>%
  count(currentMode) %>%
  mutate(prop = n/sum(n))

mode_counts <- mode_counts %>%
  mutate(type = ifelse(currentMode %in% c("AtRestaurant","AtRecreation"),
                       "Social", "Other"))

ggplot(mode_counts, aes(x = reorder(currentMode, prop), y = prop, fill = type)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Social" = "red",
    "Other" = "grey70"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Where Participants Spend Their Time",
    y = "Proportion of Time",
    x = "",
    fill = "Activity Type"
  )

# Least amount of time spent in restaurants, could it be becasue its too expensive?
```

```{r, EDA #3, warning = FALSE}
# where are social venues located across the city?

extract_xy <- function(df){
  coords <- str_extract_all(df$location, "-?\\d+\\.?\\d*")
  
  df$x <- as.numeric(sapply(coords, `[`, 1))
  df$y <- as.numeric(sapply(coords, `[`, 2))
  
  return(df)
}

pubs <- pubs %>% extract_xy()
rest <-  rest %>%extract_xy()
apartments <-  apartments %>% extract_xy()

# Mapping pub locations and their maximum occupancy 
ggplot(pubs, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = maxOccupancy), color = "darkred", alpha = .7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Pub Locations in Engagement City",
    x = "City X",
    y = "City Y"
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = pubs, aes(x, y), color = "red", alpha = .7) +
  geom_point(data = rest, aes(x, y), color = "orange", alpha = .7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Social Venues Across Engagement City")

# Useful but weak visualisations, can add more data from the particpants' info to make them more informative!
```
```{r, EDA #4, warning = FALSE}
# How accessible are social venues to the citizens?

# combine all venues
venues <- bind_rows(
  pubs %>% select(x, y),
  rest %>% select(x, y)
)

# compute the distance to nearest venue 
#install.packages('FNN')
library(FNN)

apartment_coords <- apartments %>% select(x, y)
venue_coords <- venues %>% select(x, y)

nn <- get.knnx(venue_coords, apartment_coords, k = 1)

apartments$distance_to_venue <- nn$nn.dist[,1]

# Add venues in blue to visualize how far apartments are from pubs and restaurants

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = apartments,
             aes(x = x, y = y, color = distance_to_venue),
             size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = venues,
             aes(x = x, y = y),
             color = "blue",
             size = 3) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "magma",     
    direction = -1,         
    name = "Distance to Venues"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Social Accessibility Across the City",
    x = "East–West Location",
    y = "North–South Location",
    caption = "Blue dots represent restaurants and pubs"
  )

# only one cluster of apartments is noticeably far from social venues
```



```{r, EDA #5 }
# Exploring relationship between health demographics and sociability


## Following code is to prep data for visaulisations
dt_travel <- as.data.table(travel_data)
dt_logs <- as.data.table(logs)
dt_participants <- as.data.table(participants)

# Add joviality
dt_travel <- dt_participants[, .(participantId, joviality)][dt_travel, on = "participantId"]

# Make sure timestamps are POSIXct
dt_travel[, checkOutTime := as.POSIXct(checkOutTime)]
dt_travel[, travelEndTime := as.POSIXct(travelEndTime)]
dt_travel[, tripDuration := as.numeric(difftime(travelEndTime, checkOutTime, units = "mins"))]

# Simplify purpose
dt_travel[, purposeType := ifelse(purpose == "Recreation (Social Gathering)", "Social", "Non-Social")]

```


```{r, EDA #5 contd}
dt_travel <- dt_travel %>%
  mutate(
    jov_bin = cut(joviality,
                  breaks = seq(0, 1, length.out = 10),
                  include.lowest = TRUE,
                  labels = round(seq(0, 1, length.out = 9), 1))
  )

# 2. Aggregate mean trip duration
duration_summary <- dt_travel %>%
  group_by(jov_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    avgDuration = mean(tripDuration, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3. Plot with highlight for jump
ggplot(duration_summary, aes(x = jov_bin, y = avgDuration)) +
  geom_line(group = 1, color = "orange", size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "orange") +
  # Arrow to highlight jump
  geom_segment(
    data = duration_summary %>% filter(jov_bin %in% c("0.2", "0.4")),
    aes(x = 3, xend = 4,  # numeric positions of bins 0.2 -> 0.4
        y = avgDuration[1],
        yend = avgDuration[2]),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "inches")),
    color = "blue",
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Joviality Score (binned 0–1)",
    y = "Average Trip Duration (minutes, log scale)",
    title = "Average Trip Duration by Mood",
    subtitle = "Arrow highlights the jump in trip duration from 0.2–0.4 joviality"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
